chief_complaint: A 14-year-old boy presented with a severe bacterial central nervous system infection.
history: The patient developed bacterial meningitis, ventriculitis, and communicating hydrocephalus despite broad-spectrum intravenous antibiotics and ventricular drainage. He had a systemic Streptococcus intermedius infection.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: ECG, bloods, imaging, and other tests results are not provided.
correct_diagnosis: Bacterial meningitis with ventriculitis and communicating hydrocephalus
differentials: Bacterial meningitis with ventriculitis and communicating hydrocephalus
correct_ranking: Bacterial meningitis with ventriculitis and communicating hydrocephalus
